text: A Technical Trainee performs a maintenance task on aeronautical product while being directly supervised by a task-authorised tradesperson, who is responsible for the task and certifies the completed work.
A newly hired worker with no trade qualification, part qualification or task authorisation independently performs and certifies maintenance on an aircraft component.
A tradesperson holding the relevant trade qualification and correctly task authorised performs maintenance on State aircraft.
A tradesperson holds a relevant trade qualification but has never been granted task authorisation, yet independently performs and certifies maintenance without any supervision.
A technician is authorised to perform aircraft maintenance without having completed the safety, familiarisation and application training courses.
Before authorisation, a maintainer completes the safety, familiarisation and application training courses specified in the Statement of Work.
A technician performs Explosive Ordnance aircraft maintenance (other than egress system work) without having completed the Explosive Ordnance training course.
A maintainer completes the mandatory Explosive Ordnance training course before being authorised to perform EO-related aircraft maintenance.
An NDT technician qualified to AS3669 carries out non-destructive testing only to the inspection level and methods for which they are qualified, with current annual certification.
A general engineering technician who is not qualified to AS3669 carries out non-destructive testing on an ADF aircraft component.
An NDT technician qualified only in the eddy-current method performs radiographic NDT inspection because the team was short-staffed.
An automotive spray painter who completed a DAIRMAINT-approved upgrade course performs aircraft surface finishing on ADF equipment.
A qualified automotive spray painter with no additional aircraft surface finisher training is assigned to surface finish ADF aircraft.
An ex-ADF Aircraft Surface Finisher carries out surface finishing of ADF aircraft.
An ex-ADF Electroplater carries out electroplating of ADF aircraft components.
A general workshop hand with no ADF electroplating background and without Certificate III in Engineering Production Systems (Electroplating) performs electroplating on ADF equipment.
A tradesperson holding Certificate III in Engineering Production Systems (Electroplating) performs electroplating of ADF aircraft equipment.
A civilian contractor organisation performing maintenance on Australian State aircraft within Australia is treated as subject to DEF(AUST) 9022.
A civilian maintenance organisation maintaining State aircraft in Australia claims DEF(AUST) 9022 does not apply because its staff are contractors rather than Defence civilians.
A maintainer certifies that work was performed correctly although they have not been formally task authorised for that duty.
The SMM ensures that only personnel who are proficient and formally task authorised certify completed maintenance work.
An Avionics-stream tradesperson is assigned to independently perform Mechanical-stream maintenance without having completed any of the associated Mechanical competencies.
A Mechanical-stream tradesperson completes the associated Avionics units of competency before being employed on Avionics-stream tasks.
A Structures-stream inspector signs off Avionics-stream work, relying only on their Structures training without the underpinning Avionics competencies.
A pre-1991 single-trade Armament Fitter is employed on general avionics maintenance beyond explosive-ordnance duties without obtaining the relevant avionics competencies.
A pre-1991 Armament Fitter performs explosive-ordnance-related armament tasks for which they were trained.
A Life Support Fitter who completed the RAAF Aircraft Life Support Fitter course maintains aircraft life support equipment, excluding oxygen systems.
A general aircraft mechanic with no ALS Fitter course and no Certificate III in Public Safety (Aviation Life Support Maintenance) is employed to maintain aircraft life support equipment.
A technician holding Certificate III in Public Safety (Aviation Life Support Maintenance) is employed as an Aircraft Life Support Fitter.
State aircraft are maintained by competent and authorised individuals working within an approved organisation, with their work certified as correct.
An individual maintains State aircraft outside of any approved organisation, with no certification of the work performed.
An ex-ADF METMACH carries out metal machinist specialist trade work on ADF aircraft.
A person trained by a registered training organisation that has not been approved by DAIRMAINT, DGTA-ADF is employed as a metal machinist on ADF aircraft.
NDT certification of a technician is renewed annually in accordance with the process specified by AS3669.
Aircraft surface finishing of ADF equipment is performed by a tradesperson whose upgrade course was never approved by DAIRMAINT, DGTA-ADF.
A Technical Trainee gains experience towards competency assessment by performing tasks under the direct supervision of task-authorised personnel.
A maintainer is authorised for a specific activity without completing the relevant ADF-approved off-the-job training specified in the MMP.
An Avionics tradesperson, before moving to Structures-stream tasks, first completes the associated Structures units of competency.
A Life Support Fitter performs maintenance on aircraft oxygen systems, treating them as ordinary life support equipment within their ALS scope.
A metal machinist trained by a registered training organisation approved by DAIRMAINT, DGTA-ADF carries out specialist machining work on ADF aircraft.
